$comment: D-FFI-DESCRIPTOR-INCLUDES (SQLite testfixture arc, C32 2026-07-20): the RUN witness for the TRANSITIVE shipped-header #include graph. main.c does `#include <tcl.h>` with NO direct `#include <stdio.h>` — the entire stdio surface it uses (FILE typedef, fopen/fwrite/fread/fseek/fclose/remove, SEEK_SET) arrives ONLY through tcl.json's `includes:[stdio.h]` transitive edge (real tcl.h #includes <stdio.h>; DSS resolves tcl.json, walks its includes closure, injects stdio.json's surface alongside tcl's — the flat-descriptor FILE §B fix, exactly the sqlite src/test_md5.c → tclsqlite.h → <tcl.h> path). 42 is reconstructed from two bytes round-tripped through a REAL file (fwrite then fread) so no optimizer pass can fold it away; Tcl_CreateInterp/DeleteInterp exercise the PARENT descriptor in the same TU (proving parent + transitive child coexist + grounding NEEDED libtcl8.6.so). ELF-ONLY: tcl.json is availableObjectFormats:[elf] so a pe64/macho target fails the <tcl.h> availability gate at compile (loud) — mirrors every shipped_tcl_* witness; only x86_64-linux RUNS (libtcl present; the ubuntu x86_64 CI legs install libtcl8.6, native-arm64 arch-gates/skips, windows/macos compile-only-skip via runOn). RED-ON-DISABLE: remove tcl.json's `includes` field -> FILE/fopen/... no longer injected -> main.c fails to compile with S0001 on `FILE *f;`.
